name: Ossa Da Mordere "Bones to Chew
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Almonds, blanched and coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat egg whites and sugar with lemon peel and baking powder until smoothly blended.
2. Add the nuts and stir thoroughly.
3. Squeeze the dough into tablespoon-sized lumps and shape them like short, sturdy bones.
4. Place the cookies on a greased baking sheet.
5. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 10-12 minutes or until pale brown.
6. Cool on wire racks.
7. Store in an airtight container.
NB: You don't have to shape them into bones, but it looks cute. Serving Ideas: Nice dunked into coffee or wine.
Notes: These biscuits are traditionally called "bones to chew" because they are hard and pale. They are baked on All Souls' Day in Italy.
